Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Amended Memorandum Costs By Respondent City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PETITIONER LOUISE DOUGLAS's Motion To Tax Amended Memorandum Costs By Respondent City And County Of San Francisco is GRANTED. Non-opposition filed. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Respondent City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Emery, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-4628) for Reposndent/opposing party City and County of San Francisco.
No other Appearance.

Deputy City attorney appears late after the case is called. No argument. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER LOUISE DOUGLAS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Respondent City And County Of San Francisco is GRANTED. Attorney's fees in the amount of $1,170 were imposed as a sanction against Mr. Driscoll only. These attorney's fees are not costs recoverable from the Petitioners. Mr. Driscoll is ordered to comply with May 9, 2016 order and pay $1,170 to the Respondent no later than November 23, 2016 (if the payment has not yet been made). Order is signed in open court.(see scanned order for details)Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorney James M. Emery for Respondent City and County of San Francisco the moving party.
No appearance by the opposing party.

In the matter of RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION; the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend. This writ arises out of July 17, 2015 Decision on Jurisdiction ("Decision"). The issues ripe for review are contained in the third paragraph of the Decision, specifically, (1) whether the ALJ erred in finding that the Rent Board does not have jurisdiction to determine the legality of the January 31, 2014 master lease; and (2) whether the ALJ erred in finding that the "undisputed facts show that the MOHCD continued to regulate the rents in conjunction with Mercy Housing after the prior master lease expired on January 31, 2014 whether or not it did so pursuant to a valid written lease or any lease at all." The Petition on pages 1-5 and/or pages 1-18 does not state sufficient facts to state a claim on either of these issues. Both sides appear to invite the Court to make a determination regarding the validity of the master lease on this petition (Motion 8:4-7; Opposition 2:6-9). The issue of validity of the master lease is not properly before the Court on this Petition. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)
